repo_notes.content:
This repository is a curated resource hub for the Solana Frontier Hackathon. It has three files:

- ideas.md — project ideas across five categories
- resources.md — developer tools, docs, and standards
- README.md — how to use this repo

Use these notes to route queries:

- Payments or stablecoins: see the payments note
- AI agents, agent commerce, x402, MCP: see the AI agents note
- DeFi, trading, AMMs, oracles: see the DeFi note
- Prediction markets, Kalshi, DFlow: see the prediction markets note
- Privacy, ZK proofs, stealth addresses, confidential transfers: see the privacy/ZK note
- ZK Compression, Light Token, rent-free accounts: see the ZK Compression note
- Getting started, RPC setup, wallets, frameworks, learning: see the getting started note
- NFTs: see getting started (Metaplex at developers.metaplex.com)
- Gaming: see getting started (MagicBlock at magicblock.xyz)
- Mobile: see getting started (Solana Mobile Stack at docs.solanamobile.com)

BUILDING A PAYMENT APP

Your stack: Light Token SDK for cheap account creation + Kora for gasless transactions + an embedded wallet for onboarding.

Light Token SDK (zkcompression.com/light-token/payments/overview): Creating a token account costs $0.001 instead of $0.29 with SPL. The API mirrors SPL and Token 2022. Use transferInterface — it auto-detects the token program and dispatches.

Kora (launch.solana.com/docs/kora): Users pay fees in any SPL token or pay nothing at all. Set your app as fee payer so users never touch SOL.

Wallet options:
- Embedded (smoother UX): Privy (privy.io), Para (getpara.com), Crossmint (crossmint.com)
- Crypto-native: Wallet Adapter (github.com/anza-xyz/wallet-adapter) + Phantom (phantom.app), Solflare (solflare.com), Backpack (backpack.app)

Solana Pay (solanapay.com): QR/NFC payment standard. Transfer requests for simple sends, transaction requests for custom flows. Shopify integration available.

Gotchas:
- Token Program vs Token-2022: USDC uses Token Program. PYUSD and USDG use Token-2022. Wrong program = wrong ATA address = failed transfer.
- Always validate the mint address and token program before building a transfer instruction.
- ATA creation is where cost adds up. With SPL, onboarding 10,000 users costs ~$2,900 in rent. With Light Token, ~$10.

Ideas from ideas.md:
- P2P payment app with QR/NFC scan, contact list lookup, request payments
- Split payments: group expense splitting, auto-settle in USDC
- Recurring payments: scheduled USDC transfers using token delegation
- Merchant checkout: Solana Pay integration with invoice generation and webhook callbacks
- Payroll and cross-border remittance: USDC to local currency via off-ramp, Light Token accounts for recipients with no wallet

Docs: Solana Payments (solana.com/docs/payments), Light Token SDK (zkcompression.com/light-token/payments/overview), Solana Pay (solanapay.com), Kora (launch.solana.com/docs/kora)

BUILDING WITH AI AGENTS

Your stack depends on what you're building.

Agent identity and discovery:
Solana Agent Registry (solana.com/agent-registry) gives agents on-chain identity with A2A agent cards, MCP endpoints, and capability declarations. ERC-8004 (eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8004) is a universal service registry — not just for agents. You can index uptime, response time, and success rate across MCP tool servers. It uses ERC-721 tokens whose URI resolves to a registration file.

Agent authentication:
ERC-8128 uses per-request Ed25519 HTTP signatures built on RFC 9421. No stored tokens or API keys. Keyid format: erc8128:<chainId>:<address>. The same address signs requests, registers identity on 8004, and pays via x402.

Agent commerce:
ERC-8183 (eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8183) defines a job primitive with escrow. Client funds the job, provider submits work, evaluator approves or rejects. The evaluator can be an AI agent, a ZK verifier, or a multisig. States: Open -> Funded -> Submitted -> Completed/Rejected/Expired.

Agent payments:
x402 (x402.org) and MPP (mpp.dev/overview) both use HTTP 402. Client requests a resource, server returns 402 with payment requirements, client pays and retries. x402 is by Coinbase. MPP is by Stripe and Paradigm. Both support Solana.

Agent purchase authorization:
AP2 (ap2-protocol.org) is Google's protocol for agent purchase mandates. Uses cryptographic proof of user intent via signed mandates.

Key tools:
- Solana Agent Kit (github.com/sendaifun/solana-agent-kit) — 60+ pre-built on-chain actions: DeFi, tokens, NFTs, payments
- Helius Core AI (github.com/helius-labs/core-ai) — CLI, MCP server, and agent interface for Helius and Solana
- Light Protocol Skills (github.com/Lightprotocol/skills) — payments and ZK compression skills for AI coding assistants
- Awesome Solana AI (github.com/solana-foundation/awesome-solana-ai) — curated list of agent kits, MCP servers, x402 tools
- Open Wallet Standard — multi-chain encrypted vault, keys never exposed to agent

Gotchas:
- ERC-8128 is Draft status. It exists as a GitHub PR, not merged to the EIPs repo yet.
- AP2 is early-stage with limited Solana adoption so far.
- x402 V2 changed headers to PAYMENT-REQUIRED, PAYMENT-SIGNATURE, PAYMENT-RESPONSE.
- The 8004-8183 feedback loop matters: discovery -> commerce -> reputation -> better discovery.

Ideas from ideas.md:
- Agent registry with compressed PDA reputation scores
- ERC-8004 to Solana bridge via Wormhole
- 8004 reputation aggregator for MCP servers (index uptime, response time, success rate)
- Agent-to-agent marketplace (A2A + x402)
- ERC-8128 auth middleware for Solana
- Passkey-delegated agent signer using secp256r1/WebAuthn
- ERC-8183 job marketplace on Solana
- 8183 hook library: reputation-gating, bidding, privacy, underwriting
- AP2 mandate issuer for Solana
- Visa TAP identity module

Links: x402 (x402.org), MPP (mpp.dev), AP2 (ap2-protocol.org), rick.build agentic commerce stack (rick.build/blog/agentic-commerce-infrastructure), Solana Agent Kit (github.com/sendaifun/solana-agent-kit), Solana Agent Registry (solana.com/agent-registry), intro to x402 on Solana (solana.com/developers/guides/getstarted/intro-to-x402)

BUILDING DEFI

DEX and swaps:
- Jupiter (jup.ag): Aggregates across all Solana DEXs for best price. Swap API returns serialized transactions ready to sign. Also offers limit orders, DCA, and perps.
- Orca (orca.so): Concentrated liquidity via Whirlpools. Positions are NFTs. Fees accrue and must be collected explicitly.
- Raydium (raydium.io): AMM and concentrated liquidity. AMM V4 is the most common pool type for new token launches.
- Meteora (meteora.ag): DLMM with discrete price bins — zero slippage within each bin. Dominant for memecoin and volatile token launches.

Perps, spot, and lending:
- Drift (drift.trade): Largest perp DEX on Solana. Also spot trading, lending/borrowing, prediction markets, and vaults. Open-source keeper bots for order filling and liquidations.

Oracles:
- Pyth (pyth.network): Pull-based oracle. 500+ price feeds. Sub-second latency. Data sourced from first-party market makers and exchanges.

NFTs and digital assets:
- Metaplex (developers.metaplex.com): Core NFT standard, compressed NFTs via Bubblegum, token metadata, agent registry.

Gotchas:
- Pyth is pull-based. You must post price updates yourself by calling update_price_feeds with a price update VAA. Always check publish_time for staleness.
- Raydium AMM V4 requires creating an OpenBook market before pool creation.
- Orca positions are NFTs — you need to explicitly collect accrued fees.
- DFlow (dflow.net) spans DeFi and prediction markets. For prediction market details, see the prediction markets note.

Ideas from ideas.md:
- Tokenized stock lending: Ondo/Galaxy equities as collateral for stablecoin borrowing with stock-market-aware liquidation
- Light-PDA AMM: LP positions as rent-free Light-PDAs (reference implementation at github.com/Lightprotocol/cp-swap-reference)
- RWA yield aggregator: compare yields across Ondo OUSG, Spiko SAFO, Maple on Solana. Auto-allocate by risk and duration. Expose as x402-gated API for agent treasury management.

Links: Jupiter (jup.ag), Pyth (pyth.network), Orca (orca.so), Raydium (raydium.io), Meteora (meteora.ag), Drift (drift.trade), DFlow (dflow.net), Metaplex (developers.metaplex.com)

BUILDING PREDICTION MARKETS

Your stack: DFlow API + Jupiter for swaps + Pyth for pricing.

DFlow (dflow.net) provides the first API with real tokenization of Kalshi prediction markets on Solana. Every Kalshi market is available as tokenized SPL tokens with 100% coverage. Kalshi is CFTC-regulated.

DFlow uses Concurrent Liquidity Programs (CLPs): users define intents on-chain, LPs fill at or better than the limit, protocol mints tokens for the purchased position.

Key insight: Kalshi positions are SPL tokens with $20B+ volume and 0% borrowing utilization. That is untapped DeFi collateral — nobody is lending against it yet.

PredictShark (polymark.et/product/predictshark) does parlays on Polygon. Nothing like it exists on Solana yet.

Gotchas:
- DFlow uses a multi-transaction model via CLPs, not single-swap like Jupiter.
- Winning tokens are redeemable in multiple stablecoins.
- Kalshi has a $2M grants program for applications built on top.

Ideas from ideas.md:
- Kalshi positions as lending collateral via DFlow (positions are SPL tokens with $20B+ and 0% borrowing utilization)
- Parlay engine: combine 2-4 Kalshi/DFlow positions into a single composite SPL token with multiplied odds
- Prediction market oracle: price Kalshi positions from order book data for use as DeFi collateral
- Prediction market aggregator across Kalshi, DFlow, and other sources (Capitola won $25K at the Cypherpunk hackathon with this concept)
- Agent-managed prediction portfolio: AI agent monitors markets for mispriced outcomes, rebalances via DFlow, pays for data feeds via x402, spending constrained by Light Token spend permissions

Links: DFlow (dflow.net), DFlow prediction markets API (solana.com/news/dflow-prediction-markets-api), Jupiter (jup.ag), Pyth (pyth.network)

BUILDING PRIVACY AND ZK

Your stack depends on what you're building.

Confidential transfers:
Token-2022 Confidential Balances encrypt token amounts on-chain. Combine with Light Token for scale — you get private balances at $0.001 per account instead of $0.29.
Guide: solana.com/docs/tokens/extensions/confidential-transfer

Stealth addresses:
ERC-5564 (eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-5564) defines one-time receive addresses via ECDH. Sender and receiver are unlinkable on-chain. The UX problems that blocked human adoption — key management, event scanning, fragmented balances — are trivial for agents. Stealth addresses are more practical for agent-to-agent payments than human-to-human.

Private computation:
Arcium SDK (docs.arcium.com) is an MPC framework for trustless private computation. The developer experience feels similar to Anchor.

ZK identity:
Store Groth16 proofs as compressed PDAs via ZK Compression (zkcompression.com). Prove claims like "over 18" or "passed KYC" without revealing identity.

ZK tooling:
- groth16-solana (github.com/Lightprotocol/groth16-solana) — Groth16 verifier on Solana
- Noir examples (github.com/solana-foundation/noir-examples) — Noir projects on Solana, unaudited, education only
- MagicBlock (magicblock.gg) — ephemeral rollups with TEEs for compliant privacy in program state

Gotchas:
- ERC-5564 has "Stagnant" status on Ethereum, but the cryptography is sound and well-reviewed.
- Confidential Balances are a Token-2022 extension. They do not work with the basic Token Program.
- Privacy.cash has no official frontend. Do not trust third-party frontends.

Ideas from ideas.md:
- Private stablecoin transfers with Token-2022 Confidential Balances + Light Token for scale
- ERC-5564 stealth addresses ported to Solana
- ZK identity attestation: prove claims without revealing identity, Groth16 proofs as compressed PDAs
- ZK compliance passport: one proof of AML/KYC gates access to multiple protocols
- Private .sol domain registration: commitment scheme + nullifiers to prevent double-registration
- Private agent registry: prove membership and reputation above threshold via ZK range proof without revealing which operator
- Confidential payment channel: batch N confidential transfers off-chain, settle net on-chain in one encrypted transfer for B2B

See the Privacy and ZK section in resources.md for the full tooling list.

ZK COMPRESSION AND LIGHT TOKEN — WHEN TO USE WHAT

Use Light Token when you create token accounts for users. Account creation drops from $0.29 (SPL) to $0.001. Transfers cost the same as SPL (~$0.001) but use fewer compute units: ~312 CU vs ~4,645 CU. The API mirrors SPL and Token 2022. Call transferInterface and it auto-detects the token program.

Use compressed PDAs when you need rent-free program state. Agent registries, reputation scores, identity attestations, any PDA-heavy application. A 100-byte PDA costs ~0.000015 SOL instead of 0.0016 SOL.

Don't use ZK Compression for shared state that updates very frequently within a single block (like DEX liquidity pools), accounts with far more than 1,000 lifetime writes, or accounts where you need to access more than 1KB of data in one on-chain transaction.

How it works: compressed account state is stored as calldata on Solana's ledger. Only a root hash sits on-chain in a Merkle tree. 128-byte ZK proofs verify the integrity of account data. RPC providers serve validity proofs under the hood.

Hot-cold lifecycle: active accounts stay "hot" with a small virtual rent balance bumped on each write. Inactive accounts auto-compress to "cold" after about 24 hours. Cold state is cryptographically preserved on the ledger and loads in-flight when the account is used again. You can compress and decompress at will.

Docs: zkcompression.com for Light Token and ZK Compression. solana.com/docs for Solana fundamentals.

GETTING STARTED — FIRST STEPS FOR ANY PROJECT

The recommended Solana development stack:

Client SDK: Use @solana/kit (not the legacy @solana/web3.js). Create your RPC client with createClient from @solana/kit-client-rpc. If you need legacy web3.js compatibility, use @solana/web3-compat as an adapter.

On-chain programs: Anchor (anchor-lang.com/docs) by default. It handles account serialization, security checks, and IDL generation. Use Pinocchio for performance-critical programs where you need lower compute units or smaller binary size.

Wallets: Use Wallet Standard discovery — it auto-detects installed wallets. No need to manually instantiate adapters for wallets that support the standard (Phantom, Solflare, Backpack all do). For embedded wallets (smoother onboarding): Privy (privy.io), Para (getpara.com), or Crossmint (crossmint.com).

Testing: LiteSVM or Mollusk for unit tests. Surfpool for integration tests. Use solana-test-validator only when you need full validator behavior. Default to devnet. Always simulate before sending.

RPC: Helius (helius.dev — free tier, DAS API, WebSockets), QuickNode (quicknode.com — 80+ chains), Triton (triton.one — bare metal, Yellowstone gRPC).

MCP server for AI-assisted development:
claude mcp add --transport http solana-mcp-server https://mcp.solana.com/mcp
Gives you real-time Solana docs search and expert assistance inside your coding agent.

AI and agent skills:
- npx skills add https://github.com/solana-foundation/solana-dev-skill — Solana development
- npx skills add Lightprotocol/skills — Light Token and ZK Compression
- npm install solana-agent-kit — 60+ pre-built on-chain actions
- Helius Core AI (github.com/helius-labs/core-ai) — CLI and MCP server for Helius/Solana

Learn:
- Solana docs (solana.com/docs) — accounts, transactions, programs, PDAs, CPI
- Blueshift (blueshift.gg) — free Solana courses
- Helius Blog (helius.dev/blog) — technical deep-dives
- Solana Awesome (github.com/helius-labs/solana-awesome) — curated resource list

Opt-in scaling with ZK Compression (zkcompression.com):
You don't need ZK Compression for every project. But if your app creates many accounts, it cuts costs 99%. Consider it for:
- Stablecoin payments: onboard users at $0.001/account instead of $0.29 (zkcompression.com/light-token/payments/overview)
- Token distribution and airdrops: distribute to millions without rent costs (zkcompression.com/token-distribution)
- DeFi programs: rent-free pools, vaults, LP positions, and mints (zkcompression.com/light-token/defi/programs)
- Wallets: display and swap light-tokens alongside SPL (zkcompression.com/light-token/wallets/overview)
- Data streaming: stream token and mint events via Laserstream gRPC (zkcompression.com/light-token/streaming/tokens)
- Per-user program state: profiles, credentials, DePIN nodes, nullifiers (zkcompression.com/pda/overview)
See the ZK Compression note for tradeoffs and when not to use it.

Gasless transactions: Kora (launch.solana.com/docs/kora) — users pay fees in any SPL token or not at all.

Cross-chain: Wormhole (portalbridge.com — 30+ chains), deBridge (debridge.com — 25+ chains, 0-TVL architecture).

Mobile: Solana Mobile Stack (docs.solanamobile.com) — Mobile Wallet Adapter, Seed Vault, Android SDK.

Gaming: MagicBlock (magicblock.xyz) — on-chain game engine with Ephemeral Rollups. 1ms blocks, zero fees, TEE privacy.

NFTs: Metaplex (developers.metaplex.com) — Core standard, compressed NFTs via Bubblegum.
